post_text: I built a tool that generates complete Roblox games from one sentence. tycoon with monetization. obby with checkpoints. simulator with pets. 47 seconds each. RobloxMaxx is live. here's what it does:

how it works: type 'make a pizza tycoon with 8 stations and gamepasses.' hit generate. get 15+ Luau scripts. ServerScriptService. ReplicatedStorage. StarterGui. all connected. paste into Roblox Studio. hit play. done.

what makes this different from ChatGPT: genre-specific prompts trained on real Roblox patterns. modern Luau with type annotations. proper RemoteEvent architecture. DataStore persistence. monetization hooks. ChatGPT doesn't know what a Humanoid is.
pricing: free = 250 actions/month (enough for 5+ complete games). Pro = $19/mo for 2500 actions. Studio = $49/mo for teams + priority generation. or bring your own API key and pay per token.
try it free. no credit card. robloxmaxx.com. Roblox Studio plugin + web dashboard. if you build something cool tag me. I want to see what 47 seconds of AI can produce in your hands.

built 3 Roblox games before lunch today. pizza tycoon: 47 seconds. obstacle course with 20 stages: 52 seconds. pet simulator with hatching mechanic: 61 seconds. all with gamepasses and dev products baked in. total dev time: 2 minutes 40 seconds.

RobloxMaxx doesn't write snippets. it writes the WHOLE game. ServerScriptService handlers. ReplicatedStorage modules. StarterGui interfaces. StarterPlayerScripts. all connected. all working. all from one sentence.

new template: horror game scaffold. flickering lights system. chase AI with pathfinding. jumpscare triggers. ambient sound controller. atmosphere manager. one prompt generates everything. your Roblox horror game in under 2 minutes.

just added conversation memory. tell RobloxMaxx 'add a pet system to my tycoon.' it remembers YOUR tycoon code. generates pets that integrate with your existing economy. not a new game. an upgrade to YOUR game.

people keep asking about code quality. here's a side-by-side. left: RobloxMaxx generated tycoon code. right: top-rated free model tycoon. ours has type annotations. proper cleanup. no memory leaks. no deprecated calls. judge for yourself.

template library: 8 genres. tycoon. obby. simulator. RPG. horror. racing. tower defense. fighting. each generates a complete playable game with monetization. not code snippets. full games from one prompt.
BYOK mode is live. bring your own Claude or OpenAI API key. pay per token instead of subscription. RobloxMaxx becomes the Roblox-optimized prompt layer. full control. you own the pipeline.
free tier bump: 300 actions/month. that's 6+ complete games for $0. the best marketing is people using the tool and tagging their friends. so go build something.

scaffold mode demo. I typed: 'racing game with 5 tracks, nitro boost, and a garage for car customization.' 73 seconds later: 22 scripts. vehicle physics. track system. garage UI. leaderboards. working game.

just shipped multi-turn editing. generate a tycoon. then say 'add a rebirth system.' then 'add a VIP room behind the rebirth counter.' each command builds on the last. conversational game development.

the #1 mistake in Roblox code: putting game logic in LocalScripts. RemoteEvents exist for a reason. server authoritative or get exploited. every script RobloxMaxx generates is server-authoritative by default because exploiters don't sleep.

stop copy-pasting tycoon code from 2019 YouTube tutorials. deprecated API. no type annotations. no proper cleanup. memory leaks everywhere. generate modern Luau with task.spawn and typed functions instead.

Roblox monetization pattern that works: gamepasses for permanent upgrades. dev products for consumables. put the purchase prompt AFTER the player experiences value. not before. RobloxMaxx templates follow this by default.

Roblox tip: .Destroying a part doesn't disconnect its RBXScriptConnections. always :Disconnect() or you get memory leaks that compound over time. AI-generated code handles cleanup automatically. humans forget. every time.

unpopular Roblox opinion: most devs over-engineer their games. you don't need a custom physics engine for a tycoon. you don't need 15 modules. keep it simple. ship it. iterate based on real player data. not imagined edge cases.

Roblox DataStore: always use UpdateAsync not SetAsync for player data. SetAsync overwrites without checking current value. UpdateAsync lets you merge safely. one lost save = one angry player who never comes back.

Roblox UI tip: never use Offset for positioning. always Scale. Offset breaks on different screen sizes. 60%+ of Roblox players are on mobile. if your UI uses Offset, you're broken for most of your players.

the difference between a 50-player game and a 5000-player game is almost never the gameplay. it's the first 30 seconds. loading screen. tutorial. first reward. nail those 3 things. everything else is secondary.

Roblox leaderboard code should be 8 lines. if yours is 50 you're doing it wrong. OrderedDataStore:GetSortedAsync() handles sorting. stop reinventing sorted lists. or generate it and skip the debugging entirely.
free models are not free. they cost you debugging time. backdoor risk. deprecated API calls. code you don't understand. generate clean code in 47 seconds instead. actually free. actually safe.
